summary: Completed e2e testing of Vital Signs platform with DIRECT Google OAuth (not Emergent auth). All core requirements verified: 3 stories displayed, no resonance/like buttons, Google OAuth redirects to accounts.google.com with correct client_id and HTTPS redirect_uri, JWT authentication working, admin dashboard with Post Generator tab restriction working.
seed_data_creation: Created test users in MongoDB: test-jwt-user (test@example.com, student) and admin-jwt-user (curejournal@gmail.com, admin)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth has been changed from Emergent auth to DIRECT Google OAuth with JWT tokens. JWT tokens are stored in localStorage (not cookies). Use the JWT tokens above for authenticated testing. The /api/auth/google endpoint returns 302 redirect to accounts.google.com. After Google auth, callback redirects to frontend with ?token=JWT&user=ENCODED_USER in URL.
auth_flow_notes: This is DIRECT Google OAuth, NOT Emergent auth. Auth flow: 1) Click 'Continue with Google' -> 2) Redirects to /api/auth/google -> 3) Backend redirects to accounts.google.com -> 4) After Google auth, callback redirects to frontend with ?token=JWT&user=ENCODED_USER -> 5) Frontend OAuthTokenHandler stores in localStorage -> 6) All authenticated requests use Authorization: Bearer JWT header
